_metadata.description: Phase 2 planning context — Models + Onboarding. Supabase PostgreSQL (Path B), multi-tenant schema, CRUD empresas, forced onboarding.

phase_2_scope.name: Modelos + Onboarding
phase_2_scope.objective: Database models, Alembic migrations, multi-tenant companies CRUD API, user-company role associations (FK to auth.users), forced onboarding flow on frontend.

deliverables.description: SQLAlchemy 2.0 declarative models: profiles, companies, company_members, plus audit-phase tables (evaluations, answers, sections, questions, scores, recommendations, action_items, share_links)
Alembic autogenerate migration creating all tables with correct FKs, constraints, and indexes
FastAPI CRUD for /api/companies (POST, GET, PATCH, DELETE — RESTRICT delete) with tenant-scoped queries
API endpoints for managing company members (user-company role associations)
Frontend: forced onboarding flow after login if user has no company; company creation form; company selection; role management UI

acceptance_criteria: alembic upgrade head creates all tables with correct schema
POST /api/companies creates a company and owner role for the authenticated user
GET /api/companies returns only companies the user belongs to
Frontend onboarding: new user sees company creation form immediately after login; existing user sees dashboard

architectural_decision_path_b.title: ARCHITECTURAL DECISION: Path B — Supabase Managed PostgreSQL
architectural_decision_path_b.summary: NO local Docker PostgreSQL. Supabase project `asgofjauortyigsoliii` provides BOTH Auth + PostgreSQL. The `profiles` table FKs directly to `auth.users(id)` — no shadow `auth_users` table needed.

supabase_project._security_note: ANON_KEY is safe for context store — it is the public anon key used in frontend. SERVICE_ROLE_KEY is never stored here.

auth_schema_relationship.mechanism: FK from public.profiles.id → auth.users.id (NO shadow table)
auth_schema_relationship.profiles_table_notes: profiles is the app-level extension of auth.users. It stores display_name, avatar_url, preferences — anything NOT provided by Supabase Auth's built-in users table.
auth_schema_relationship.migration_notes: CREATE TABLE profiles (id UUID PRIMARY KEY REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT, ...)

current_state.frontend_auth: Working — Zustand + localStorage for mock data, Supabase login/register functional

data_model_changes_from_audit._source: Previous agent audit (db-designer) — design decisions that MUST be followed

decisions.rule: ALL foreign keys use ON DELETE RESTRICT
decisions.rationale: Preserve audit trails; never cascade deletes

decisions.rule: answers.response must be ENUM (yes/no/partial/na), NOT BOOLEAN
decisions.rationale: NA requires exclusion from denominator; partial requires fractional scoring

decisions.rule: evaluations.finished_at must be NULLABLE (no DEFAULT now())
decisions.rationale: NULL means in-progress; only set when status transitions to completed

decisions.rule: Add missing tables: sections, scores, recommendations, action_items, share_links
decisions.rationale: These were in PLAN.md data model but missing from initial schema consideration

decisions.rule: All columns must have NOT NULL where appropriate
decisions.rationale: Data integrity — nullable only when genuinely optional

decisions.rule: Use NUMERIC(5,2) NOT DOUBLE PRECISION for scores
decisions.rationale: Exact decimal arithmetic — no floating-point rounding issues in compliance percentages

decisions.rule: ENUM types for role (admin/auditor/reader), status (in_progress/completed), sector, size
decisions.rationale: Native PostgreSQL enums for type safety and constraint enforcement

decisions.rule: Composite UNIQUE on company_members(company_id, profile_id) and answers(evaluation_id, question_id)
decisions.rationale: No duplicate memberships; upsert answers by (evaluation, question)

decisions.rule: Add updated_at to ALL mutable tables
Add indexes on ALL foreign key columns
decisions.rationale: Query performance for joins and lookups

plan_vs_frontend_conflict._status: UNRESOLVED — MUST ASK USER BEFORE IMPLEMENTING QUESTIONNAIRE
plan_vs_frontend_conflict.resolution_required: BEFORE implementing models, seed data, questionnaire API, or assessment flow. This affects: questions table schema, answers.response ENUM values, scoring logic, frontend questionnaire components.

table_schema_phase2._note: Tables needed for Phase 2. Focus on companies + profiles + membership. Other tables (sections, questions, etc.) defined but may be populated in Phase 3.

columns.name: id
columns.constraints: PK, REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT

columns.name: company_id
columns.constraints: NOT NULL, FK → companies.id ON DELETE RESTRICT

columns.name: profile_id
columns.constraints: NOT NULL, FK → profiles.id ON DELETE RESTRICT

implementation_order: 1. Define PostgreSQL ENUM types in Alembic (role_enum, sector_enum, size_enum, etc.)
4. Create company_members table with composite UNIQUE
5. Create remaining tables (sections, questions, evaluations, answers, scores, recommendations, action_items, share_links)
10. Frontend: onboarding flow (CompanyCreatePage, CompanySwitchPage, forced redirect logic)

conventions_reminder.commits: feat(phase-2): add companies CRUD with tenant scoping
conventions_reminder.backend_path: backend/app/ — all new models in backend/app/models/
conventions_reminder.migrations: NEVER edit committed Alembic revision; always new revision
conventions_reminder.tenancy: ALL queries must be tenant-scoped (company_id from JWT/context)
conventions_reminder.deletes: RESTRICT — return 409 if dependencies exist, never cascade
